Stanford University
GPS Lab Aeronautics & Astronautics
Home People Research Publications Resources News Contact

Resources

Browse

Presentations

PowerPoint and PDF presentations covering GPS, GNSS, WAAS, LAAS, integrity monitoring, and related research topics.

Software Tools

Software developed by the Stanford GPS Lab, including MAAST, SUGL Ephemeris Files, Gaussian Overbounding tools, and the Stanford GPS/GNSS MATLAB Platform.

MAAST

MATLAB Algorithm Availability Simulation Tool (MAAST) for WAAS and ARAIM availability simulations.

SUGL Ephemeris Files

Processed GPS navigation ephemeris files generated by the Stanford GPS Laboratory for research applications.

Gaussian Overbounding

MATLAB implementation of the two-step Gaussian overbounding algorithm described in the Stanford GPS Lab's integrity research.

Stanford GPS/GNSS MATLAB Platform

Integrated MATLAB research platform supporting numerous GNSS data formats including RINEX, NMEA, and NSTB.

A Celebration of Professor Per Enge's Life

Memorial page including a photo collage and celebration videos honoring Professor Per Enge.

GPS-Related Website Links

Curated collection of GPS, GNSS, FAA, ION, Stanford, and international navigation research resources.

SBAS Ionospheric Working Group

Resources and publications on ionospheric scintillation and its effects on Satellite-Based Augmentation Systems.

GPS Lab Alumni

Directory of former Stanford GPS Lab researchers, students, and alumni.
